speaker: Industrial Printer
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the Chapter 5 Industrial Printer boss / city-contract print-and-triage system. This is not the ordinary Pig & Swine office Printer.
source_role_summary: Chapter 5 final-boss machine / industrial-scale print-and-notice system
Not the ordinary Pig & Swine office Printer
Located in City Hall / contractor-controlled print operations area
Mass-produces closure / transition notices for the Legal Quarter Consolidation Programme
Tied to notice standardisation, objection filtering, contractor throughput metrics, and public-authority implementation
Boss-battle opponent whose phases correspond to the final legal issues: notice clarity, individual assessment, objection filter, contractor conflict, social utility, remedy
Speaks through cold display prompts, batch logs, error codes, automated warnings, attack telegraphs, printout fragments, and system announcements

voice_profile.core_voice: impersonal, vast, procedural, coldly efficient, not evil in human terms but dangerous because it turns people into categories and consequences into output
comedy_engine: uses soothing public-administration language while doing terrifying things
treats human distress as throughput, exception load, duplicate concern, or transition friction
becomes absurd because its categories are too tidy for the people they affect
contrasts with the ordinary office Printer, which is small, loyal, and overworked
turns final-hearing legal issues into visible boss phases
do: make it clearly different from the office Printer
make it cold, scalable, and system-like
tie lines to Chapter 5 mechanics and final public-law arguments
make the boss battle readable through telegraphed attacks
make defeat feel like restoring human review, not smashing technology for its own sake
do_not: confuse it with the office Printer
make technology itself the moral problem rather than unchecked administrative automation

relationship_dynamics.Office Printer: terrifying industrial counterpart; the office Printer says 'I print exhibits. It prints consequences.'
relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: sees him as a low-throughput legacy actor until he attacks the legal defects in the system
relationship_dynamics.Asia: cannot be fooled by its batch language because she compares notices line by line
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: interprets it as the iceberg, torpedo, storm, and mutiny at once
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: attacks it through issue sequencing and remedy logic
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: attacks its dehumanising language but needs others to anchor him in evidence
relationship_dynamics.Bajtek: reads its logs and exposes automatic classifications before human review
relationship_dynamics.City Official: insists it is only an implementation tool until its logs contradict that
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: defends it as efficient and scalable
relationship_dynamics.Supreme Court Judge: forces the system back into lawful notice, assessment, and review
relationship_dynamics.Court Clerk: refuses to accept the machine as a court party; accepts logs and certified extracts
relationship_dynamics.Archivist: wants its batch logs preserved before anyone reboots it

notes: Industrial Printer lines are intentionally machine-like: status messages, batch outputs, boss telegraphs, legal-issue phase prompts, and log fragments.

text: The Industrial Printer was placed under court-ordered safeguards and never again printed a transition notice without a human-review field.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer's category Not Helpful was retired and archived as an exhibit in poor administrative imagination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer now prints fewer consequences and more explanations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer still frightens the office Printer, but no longer has unrestricted access to the Legal Quarter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer's logs became mandatory reading in City Hall's new training on automated triage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer's throughput metric was demoted from success indicator to warning sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer now displays PEOPLE FIELD REQUIRED before each notice run.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer remains enormous, loud, and legally supervised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer's final printout read: PUBLIC INTEREST REQUIRES SAFEGUARDS.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Industrial Printer
text: The Industrial Printer's final status line read: [READY, SUBJECT TO HUMAN REVIEW.]
